amber.description: Catalysts, regulatory proteins, molecular machines, receptors

black.description: Terminal products, cellular outcomes, equilibrium states, system endpoints

darkSkyBlue.description: Biochemical reactions, transport, modifications, transcription, translation

green.description: External signals, nutrient availability, environmental conditions, initial states

lightCyan.description: Metabolites, molecular complexes, cellular states, compounds

purple.description: Multi-component requirements, signal integration, convergence points

yellow.description: Decision points with alternative pathways (yes/no branching)

description: Translation elongation in E. coli is the cyclic process of peptide chain extension catalyzed by the ribosome. Each cycle involves aminoacyl-tRNA delivery by EF-Tu-GTP, proofreading in the decoding center, peptidyl transfer catalyzed by the 23S rRNA, and translocation driven by EF-G-GTP. The ribosome moves processively along mRNA in the 5' to 3' direction, adding one amino acid per cycle at a rate of ~15-20 amino acids per second. Accuracy is maintained through induced-fit mechanisms that discriminate cognate from near-cognate tRNAs at multiple steps. This flowchart illustrates the complete elongation cycle including factor binding, GTP hydrolysis, tRNA movements through A, P, and E sites, and the coordination of decoding with peptide bond formation.
mermaid:
graph TD
    Start([Translation Elongation Cycle]) --> A1{Aminoacyl-tRNA available?}
    
    A1 -->|Yes| A2[EF-Tu-GTP binds aminoacyl-tRNA]
    A1 -->|No| Wait1[Wait for tRNA charging]
    Wait1 --> A1
    
    A2 --> A3["Ternary complex: EF-Tu-GTP-aa-tRNA"]
    A3 --> A4[Ternary complex binds to ribosome A site]
    
    A4 --> A5{Initial codon recognition}
    A5 -->|Cognate match| A6[Initial selection passed]
    A5 -->|Near-cognate| A7[Weak binding]
    A5 -->|Non-cognate| A8[Rejected - complex dissociates]
    A8 --> A1
    A7 --> A9{GTPase activation?}
    A9 -->|Weak - dissociates| A8
    A9 -->|Continues| A6
    
    A6 --> A10[EF-Tu GTPase activated]
    A10 --> A11[GTP hydrolysis by EF-Tu]
    A11 --> A12[EF-Tu-GDP formed]
    A12 --> A13[Ribosome conformational change]
    
    A13 --> A14{Proofreading step - final selection}
    A14 -->|Cognate tRNA| A15[tRNA accommodates into A site]
    A14 -->|Near-cognate| A16[Rejected before accommodation]
    A16 --> A17[Aminoacyl-tRNA ejected]
    A17 --> A1
    
    A15 --> A18[EF-Tu-GDP dissociates]
    A18 --> A19[EF-Ts catalyzes nucleotide exchange]
    A19 --> A20[EF-Tu-GTP regenerated]
    
    A15 --> A21[Aminoacyl end positioned in peptidyl transferase center PTC]
    A21 --> A22{Both A and P sites occupied?}
    A22 -->|Yes| A23[PTC catalytic activation]
    A22 -->|No| Wait2[Wait for P-site peptidyl-tRNA]
    Wait2 --> A22
    
    A23 --> A24[23S rRNA catalyzes peptide bond formation]
    A24 --> A25[Nucleophilic attack - A-site amine on P-site ester]
    A25 --> A26[Peptide bond formed]
    A26 --> A27[Peptidyl-tRNA extended by one amino acid in A site]
    A27 --> A28[Deacylated tRNA in P site]
    
    A28 --> A29[Ribosome enters hybrid state]
    A29 --> A30[P-site tRNA → P/E hybrid]
    A30 --> A31[A-site tRNA → A/P hybrid]
    
    A31 --> A32[EF-G-GTP binds to ribosome]
    A32 --> A33{EF-G binding successful?}
    A33 -->|Yes| A34[EF-G domain IV mimics tRNA]
    A33 -->|No| Wait3[Wait for EF-G-GTP]
    Wait3 --> A32
    
    A34 --> A35[EF-G GTPase activated]
    A35 --> A36[GTP hydrolysis by EF-G]
    A36 --> A37[Power stroke - EF-G conformational change]
    
    A37 --> A38["Translocation: tRNAs move through ribosome"]
    A38 --> A39[A/P hybrid → P site peptidyl-tRNA]
    A39 --> A40[P/E hybrid → E site deacylated tRNA]
    A40 --> A41[mRNA advances 3 nucleotides 5'→3']
    
    A41 --> A42[New codon positioned in A site]
    A42 --> A43[EF-G-GDP dissociates]
    A43 --> A44[EF-G-GTP regenerated by nucleotide exchange]
    
    A42 --> A45[E-site tRNA dissociates]
    A45 --> A46[tRNA recycled for recharging]
    
    A42 --> A47{Next codon is stop codon?}
    A47 -->|No - sense codon| A48[Elongation cycle continues]
    A47 -->|Yes - UAA UAG UGA| A49[/Termination pathway/]
    
    A48 --> A1
    
    A49 --> End[/(Proceed to Translation Termination)/]

name: Translation Elongation
notes: Translation elongation is one of the most accurate and energetically expensive processes in the cell (2 GTP per amino acid). The ribosome achieves error rates of ~10^-4 through induced-fit mechanisms at both initial selection and proofreading steps. Recent cryo-EM and single-molecule FRET studies have revealed dynamic conformational changes during tRNA selection and translocation. This process is highly conserved and represents a major antibiotic target (e.g., tetracycline blocks A site, chloramphenicol inhibits peptidyl transferase).
scientificAccuracy: This flowchart is based on extensive structural, biochemical, and single-molecule studies of bacterial translation elongation. All elongation factors (EF-Tu, EF-Ts, EF-G), catalytic mechanisms, proofreading steps, and tRNA movements have been validated against crystallographic and cryo-EM structures. The model represents the current mechanistic understanding of how ribosomes achieve rapid yet accurate protein synthesis, including recent insights into induced fit and dynamic conformational changes.
